summary: Machine policy for limiting advanced diagnostic log collection. The local Microsoft DataCollection.admx file explicitly documents the same DataCollection policy key and LimitDiagnosticLogCollection value name that the app uses, and the ADML help text explains the effect.

setting.name: Advanced Diagnostic Log Collection
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can collect extra diagnostic logs when advanced diagnostics are involved. This policy decides whether that additional log collection is limited.
setting.why_people_change_it: Privacy-oriented users and managed environments may want to reduce how much advanced diagnostic logging can be collected.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Limiting advanced diagnostic logs can reduce the amount of collected data, but it can also lower the quality of diagnostic evidence available for deeper troubleshooting.
setting.professional_notes: DataCollection.admx maps this machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\LimitDiagnosticLogCollection with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force this diagnostic-log limitation policy.

allowed_values.label: Do not limit advanced diagnostic logs
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and advanced diagnostic logging is not restricted by this setting.

allowed_values.label: Limit advanced diagnostic logs
allowed_values.meaning: Advanced diagnostic log collection is limited by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes LimitDiagnosticLogCollection = 1, which matches the documented limit behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 2004 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This describes only the policy baseline and not every other telemetry setting that could affect diagnostics.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that may need the broadest diagnostic detail
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal policy baseline for advanced diagnostics.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to reduce extra diagnostic-log collection.
tradeoffs: Advanced diagnostics are not restricted by this policy.
Potentially more diagnostic detail can remain available.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
intended_for: Managed environments with tighter diagnostic controls
avoid_for: Scenarios where maximum diagnostic detail is important

states.rationale: Limit advanced diagnostic log collection by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to reduce the amount of advanced diagnostic log data that can be collected.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need deeper diagnostic evidence for support or engineering analysis.
tradeoffs: Diagnostic data collection is reduced for this category.
Some advanced troubleshooting detail may be unavailable.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: LimitDiagnosticLogCollection
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that limits advanced diagnostic log collection.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows LimitDiagnosticLogCollection with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the DataCollection policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes LimitDiagnosticLogCollection = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft DataCollection.admx file explicitly defines LimitDiagnosticLogCollection under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection with valueName LimitDiagnosticLogCollection, and the paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy stops additional diagnostic logs from being collected while disabled or not configured may allow them. The app writes that documented limiting value.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same DataCollection policy key and LimitDiagnosticLogCollection value used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy prevents additional diagnostic logs from being collected.
